arts.ttl: Chris Davies opens up on Alfie May axe as Jay Stansfield takes the lead for Birmingham City
arts.sum: Chris Davies has empathised with Alfie May after dropping the Birmingham City striker in favour of a free-scoring Jay Stansfield. “From Alfie’s point of view, he scored a couple of really good goals against Wigan that set us up well in the league, then I made a couple of changes for the cup games, bits and bobs, and as it’s all panned out he’s played the Wrexham game but he’s not been starting games,” said Davies.

arts.ttl: I couldn’t walk into my local when I played for Birmingham City - I would have got shot - Birmingham Live
arts.sum: Geoff Horsfield recounts his experiences playing for Birmingham City in Second City derbies against Aston Villa Geoff Horsfield claims he couldn’t set foot in his local pub when he played for Birmingham City through fear of getting shot.

arts.ttl: Saturday's League One predictions including Birmingham City vs. Charlton Athletic

arts.ttl: Our writers agree on Birmingham City XI set for Charlton revenge mission
arts.sum: (4-2-3-1): Allsop; Laird, Klarer, Davies, Cochrane; Iwata, Paik; Dowell, Willumsson, Anderson; Stansfield Charlton are one of the form teams in League One over the past six games, having taken 16 points from a possible 18 – a record Blues have matched.

arts.ttl: Birmingham City Women: St Andrew's struggles, a double award and internationals
arts.sum: A round-up of the latest Birmingham City Women news before the Championship clash with Sheffield United this weekend Birmingham City Women are back at St Andrew’s @ Knighthead Park this weekend to take on Championship strugglers Sheffield United.

arts.sum: Chris Davies gives Christoph Klarer injury update before Birmingham City battle Charlton On Gardner-Hickman, Davies commented: “He’s getting there.

arts.ttl: Nathan Jones makes Birmingham City admission after refuelling Alfie May fire
arts.sum: Charlton are one of two sides to defeat Blues in League One this season and have taken 16 points from their last six matches to move into the play-off places. Nathan Jones brings his in-form Charlton side to St Andrew's this weekend to take on League One leaders Birmingham City

arts.ttl: Charlton Athletic receive selection boost ahead of Birmingham City trip
arts.sum: Charlton Athletic boss Nathan Jones has said Thierry Small has fully ‘recovered’ following Emmanuel Fernandez’s tackle last time… The post Charlton Athletic receive selection boost ahead of Birmingham City trip appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Nathan Jones delivers assessment on his first 12 months in charge of Charlton Athletic
arts.sum: “The progress has been very realistic – both for a club of our stature and size but also where we came from,” Jones told the South London Press . “The club and the team are in a really good position considering we’ve had a lot of new signings and that when we came in we were just outside the relegation zone.
